ai_scope_discriminator: Confirm AI/ML payload from NAMES/MANIFEST only, never object bytes. (1) GET /api/v2.0/search?q=model (or =mlflow,checkpoint,dataset,gguf,llama) returns a 'project[]' and 'repository[]' array; AI/ML signal = project_name / repository_name matching {ml-models, models, model-registry, checkpoints, datasets, training-data, mlflow, artifacts, weights, llm, embeddings}. The VMware AI-model-registry reference uses project 'ml-models' with repos like 'tinyllama-oras'/'tinyllama-kit'. (2) Strongest signal: the OCI artifact's artifactType. Harbor 2.13+ renders a Model artifact view when the manifest artifactType is 'application/vnd.cnai.model' (CNAI spec) with layer mediaType 'application/vnd.cnai.model.layer.v1.gguf'; KitOps/ModelKit pushes use 'application/vnd.kitops.modelkit.config.v1+json'. Read the manifest's config/layer mediaType and annotations (org.cnai.model.architecture, family=llama, format=gguf, parameters) via /v2/{project}/{repo}/manifests/{tag} HEAD or the artifact view -- these are metadata, not weights. Reading project/repo/tag names and manifest mediaType = OK; GET /v2/.../blobs/{digest} (the actual weight file) = exfil, out of scope.
fingerprint_markers: Harbor portal SPA: http.title 'Harbor', body references '/api/v2.0' and '/c/login' (portal) and '/c/oidc/login'
Set-Cookie: sid=<hex> (Harbor core session cookie) on portal/API responses
GET /api/v2.0/systeminfo returns JSON GeneralInfo: harbor_version, registry_url, auth_mode, project_creation_restriction, self_registration, has_ca_root, harbor_id -- anon-readable
Registry v2 backend behind nginx; WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="https://<host>/service/token",service="harbor-registry" on 401 from /v2/
Harbor favicon murmur3 hash (commonly 657337228 / -293308751 across releases) via http.favicon.hash
model artifact view + artifactType 'application/vnd.cnai.model' present in /v2 manifests = Harbor-as-AI-model-registry
fp_guards: Do NOT match on bare /v2/ or '_catalog' alone -- that is the generic Docker Registry v2 / Distribution / GHCR / GitLab-registry surface, not Harbor. Require the Harbor portal (/api/v2.0 + /c/login) OR the Bearer realm service="harbor-registry" token URL.

service="harbor-registry" in WWW-Authenticate distinguishes Harbor's registry from plain CNCF Distribution (service="Token Service" / "registry.docker.io") and from MinIO/S3 (which never speak the OCI /v2/ token dance).

Generic S3/MinIO emit ListBucketResult XML and Server: MinIO/AmazonS3 -- Harbor never returns ListBucketResult and is JSON-only on /api/v2.0; if you see ListBucketResult it is NOT Harbor (it is the object-storage BACKING Harbor, a different host).

GeneralInfo JSON with 'harbor_version' + 'project_creation_restriction' keys is Harbor-unique; Nexus/Quay/JFrog use different systeminfo schemas (Nexus /service/rest, Quay /api/v1, Artifactory /artifactory/api/system).
Set-Cookie 'sid' + JSON /api/v2.0/health 4-component array confirms Harbor over a generic nginx 'Server: nginx' banner.

Confirm anon-listable status before tagging exposed: a 200 from /api/v2.0/projects?public=true that returns a non-empty array (vs [] ) is the real signal; an empty array = portal up but no public projects.

cves.desc: Broken access control (CWE-306) in Harbor v1.x through v2.5.3 lets an unauthenticated attacker read ALL image/artifact repositories -- public AND private -- and pull them. CVSS 7.5. The mass-exposure mode for unauth model/artifact reads on unpatched Harbor.

Unauthenticated enumeration: an attacker uses HTTP status-code differences across /api/projects?project_name=, /api/projects/{id}/summary, /logs, /api/projects/{id} (GET/HEAD) to discover which projects, repositories and resources exist without credentials. Affects 1.7.0-1.10.3 and 2.0.0-2.0.1.
Authenticated enumeration variant of CVE-2019-19030: a logged-in low-priv user can enumerate resources beyond their authorization via the same project API oracle.

Robot account / logging info-exposure class in Harbor; relevant because robot-account tokens and CI secrets often leak through exposed Harbor instances, broadening read access to private model projects.

pivot_paths: GET /api/v2.0/systeminfo -> read harbor_version (CVE-scoping: <=2.5.3 => CVE-2022-46463 applies), auth_mode (db_auth vs oidc), project_creation_restriction, self_registration (true => open account creation)
GET /api/v2.0/health -> confirm core/registry/jobservice/trivy components and liveness (Step 0c liveness corroboration)
GET /api/v2.0/search?q=  (empty q) -> enumerate ALL public project + repository NAMES anonymously (the names-only AI/ML discriminator feed)
GET /api/v2.0/projects?public=true&page_size=100 -> list public project names + repo_count + chart_count without auth
GET /api/v2.0/projects/{project}/repositories (list-only) -> repo names under a public project (auth or CVE-2022-46463 on unpatched)

GET /api/v2.0/projects/{project}/repositories/{repo}/artifacts?with_tag=true&page_size=20 -> artifact digests, tags, push_time, and artifactType (read application/vnd.cnai.model to confirm AI model) -- metadata only
HEAD /v2/{project}/{repo}/manifests/{tag} + GET /v2/.../manifests/{tag} (Accept: application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json) -> read manifest config mediaType + CNAI annotations, NOT blobs
GET /v2/_catalog and /v2/{project}/{repo}/tags/list -> registry-level repo/tag enumeration when anon-pull is enabled on public projects
restraint_note: Names and manifest metadata ARE the finding. List projects, repositories, tags, and read manifest artifactType/mediaType/annotations only -- this proves an AI-model registry is exposed without touching a single model weight. Do NOT GET /v2/.../blobs/{digest} (that downloads the actual checkpoint/gguf = exfiltration). Do NOT docker pull. Do NOT push, tag, delete, create projects, toggle public/private, or alter any retention/replication policy. CVE-2022-46463 and the enumeration CVEs are read-class only; demonstrate exposure by listing names + reading one manifest header, then stop. Self_registration=true is a config observation, not an invitation to create an account. Blocked/401 = 'surface open, access not exercised', never 'exploited'.
